name: Cefalotin
indication: Used to prevent infection during surgery and to treat many kinds of infections of the blood, bone or joints, respiratory tract, skin, and urinary tract.
mechanismOfAction: The bactericidal activity of cefalotin results from the inhibition of cell wall synthesis via affinity for penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs). The PBPs are transpeptidases which are vital in peptidoglycan biosynthesis. Therefore, their inhibition prevents this vital cell wall compenent from being properly synthesized.
pharmacodynamics: Cefalotin (INN) or cephalothin (USAN) is a semisynthetic first generation cephalosporin having a broad spectrum of antibiotic activity that is administered parenterally.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Cefalotin.

interactions.name: Picosulfuric acid
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Picosulfuric acid can be decreased when used in combination with Cefalotin.

interactions.name: Warfarin
interactions.description: Cefalotin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Warfarin.

interactions.name: Dicoumarol
interactions.description: Cefalotin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Dicoumarol.

interactions.name: Phenindione
interactions.description: Cefalotin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenindione.

interactions.name: Phenprocoumon
interactions.description: Cefalotin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenprocoumon.

interactions.name: Acenocoumarol
interactions.description: Cefalotin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Acenocoumarol.

interactions.name: Ethyl biscoumacetate
interactions.description: Cefalotin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Ethyl biscoumacetate.

interactions.name: Fluindione
interactions.description: Cefalotin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Fluindione.

interactions.name: Clorindione
interactions.description: Cefalotin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Clorindione.

interactions.name: Diphenadione
interactions.description: Cefalotin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Diphenadione.

interactions.name: Tioclomarol
interactions.description: Cefalotin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Tioclomarol.

interactions.name: Probenecid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Cefalotin can be increased when it is combined with Probenecid.
